metadata.name: Jellyfish #111
metadata.description:
Sea, sea depths and jelly in them. 
Jellyfish in my eyes, but in yours who knows what.

Press 's' in live mode for 2000x2000 png export.

Made with p5js, June, 2022.

metadata.name: Weave #55
metadata.description: Generative artwork made with p5.js

metadata.name: Sintra #51
metadata.description:
A collection of abstract architectural pieces slowly animated in realtime.

Each token produce a unique output procedurally generated based on defined rarity traits.

Generative artwork by compute collective (@computenft)

metadata.name: PixlFlippr #483
metadata.description:
PixlFlippr is an offshoot of my "Simple Harmonic Motion" series, inspired by the complexity of life emerging through the interaction of simple 'dumb' behaviors, with intricate details emerging on multiple scales. PixlFlippr operates on a pixel level (in the form of a GLSL frag shader).

Every pixel oscillates at a single fixed frequency, slightly out-of-sync with its neighbors. There is no explicit pixel-neighborhood communication (as there would be in eg Cellular Automata), and every pixel acts autonomously, oblivious to its surroundings. Yet collectively, the entire system exhibits constantly evolving complex high level behavior on many scales, ranging from patterns & behaviors spanning a few pixels, all the way up to the level of the entire image. These are further amplified through the emergent pixel aliasing artefacts & moiré patterns. 

Also, it's trippy af. 

PS All generative mints are a seamless loop with a loop duration ranging from a few minutes to over an hour. So stick around & watch it mutate!

metadata.name: Caught in a Void #108
metadata.description:
A reflection on the 571 days between the start of the COVID lockdown and quitting my job to be a generative artist.

Features include silhouette type, color palette, and fill effect.

p5.js

metadata.name: OpenBLOCK #167
metadata.description:
OpenBLOCK is a collection of open architectures. 
An architecture machine, creating endless open configurations. 
Each minted OpenBLOCK is a part of the whole, an open form, constructed using a kit of parts in various deterministic tectonic configurations, lights, and moods.

OpenBLOCK Generative Token by @grasser_alex 2021

metadata.name: Garden, Monoliths #137
metadata.description:
"Garden, Monoliths" is the result of an algorithm that started in April 2021. It was called "Grass.js".
Spring was blooming all around the house, taking over the stones and remnants of ancient battles that stood here. Inspiration was everywhere.
The initial goal was to use mathematical formulas for creating patterns inspired by the density of nature, that could be used by a pen plotter, so it kept focusing on outputting vector lines. This history and practice produced a graphical style based on line strokes, plus occasionally some transparent, filled shapes.
Each artwork will be either "Garden" or "Monolith", for they are variations of two existing designs from the workshop.

metadata.name: Dragons #292
metadata.description:
A grid of many lines forming a dragon crafted on paper.
They all have their own personality... Be sure to name and take care of them.

tw: williamapan

metadata.name: Genuary 2 #100
metadata.description: Made with code and ❤️ in 10 minutes by pifragile.

metadata.name: {Lumina} #1642
metadata.description:
The restitution of a breath
Your breath is your artwork

{Lumina} is part of the installation Σ Lumina taking place at Musée d’Orsay. A free live minting is scheduled from February 13th to March 10th, 2024. 

Breath, the vital essence, embodies our interconnection. It carries our shared heritage. A medium that helps Σ Lumina to unite the physical and the digital, the living with the coded. The voices of Orsay resonate within us, their breath becoming ours, landscaping our common creation.


Art curators of {Le Code d’Orsay}:
Christophe Leribault,
Président de l’Établissement public du musée d’Orsay et du musée de l’Orangerie
Virginie Donzeaud, Administratrice générale adjointe

16x16
HTML, js, Webgl
Location: Musée d’Orsay

metadata.name: Splash Funk #3
metadata.description:
The Splash Funk collection is based on moving particles in a 3 dimensional space that have as their starting position a point on either a sphere, a cube or a pyramid. The particles will then follow an algorithmically defined movement which is captured after a certain time period. 

Generative Art by pifragile.
pifragile.com
@pifragile

metadata.name: rect-on-rect #50
metadata.description:
glsl + p5js
each piece will run infinitely, never repeating itself
fully responsive
best performance in google chrome

metadata.name: Alienpillars #77
metadata.description:
"A rare type of bug that supposedly arrived on a cargo ship is destroying crops on planet Uctumi, in the binary system of Extronium. A wide variety of very resistant subspecies has mutated and it is necessary to study and identify all of them to obtain a gen-activated pesticide that will work on all of their variations. The pest department of the Uctumi central government is working hard on it, and they say that these strange critters are probably related to similar species that inhabited the planet Earth of the Solar system a long time ago and have spread over the galaxy."

My second piece using the p5.js raymarcher I wrote. The drawing is made of circles and lines based on the 3D data. I wanted to try something hairy as it's easy to draw lines aligned to the surface normal with this method. I also used a shader for the frame and the glitchy/sketch features.

Features 

body color: green-red-purple-yellow-blue-black
body tone: dark-light
spots color: orange-green-blue-yellow-white
hair tips: yes-no
hair #1: short-medium-long
hair #2: short-medium-long
head size: small-medium-large
head shape: long-spherical-wide
glitchy mode: 10% chance
sketch mode: 9% chance

metadata.name: Heliks #55
metadata.description:
To bid farewell to fx(hash) 1.0, I prepared this piece based on the intro "Random Generation", which I coded for the demogroup Latitude Independent Association and was awarded second place in the 4k intro compo at the Revision 2023 demoparty. 

The centerpiece is an interpretation of a strange star surrounded by helicoidal surfaces, animated with a smooth and steady motion, contributing to a sense of harmony and continuity.

This work was designed and developed using various techniques within a fragment shader, allowing me to explore and experiment with the properties of light and motion. My aim was to create something that captured both the dynamic energy of the surface and the elegance of the moving helicoidal surfaces, always striving to maintain a balance between technical complexity and visual aesthetics.

The piece is somewhat demanding on the GPU and is not designed for mobile devices or old, low-end GPUs.

metadata.name: Acequia #305
metadata.description:
Acequia (“ah-seh-kee-uh”) - a Spanish adaptation of the Arabic word as-sāqiya, meaning “water carrier”. 

In our second collaboration, Rich and Rick are back with our blend of generative code, intricate design, attention to detail, and a splash of charm. 

 Acequias are traditional irrigation canals used to distribute rainwater and snowmelt along a complex network of narrow channels. They are found in Spain, the American Southwest, and northern Mexico, where they are essential for sustaining fields, orchards, and gardens in semi-arid regions.   
Our aim in this series is to capture the essence of these waterways in bold geometric designs. Each piece is inspired by a mountain village or town in Andalusia (southern Spain). 

The location determines the cube designs and construction rules that are used to build the final output. These are combined with striking day/night palettes, outline styles, scales, climates, and a few surprises of course!


Controls (in live view): 

- Press [p] to pause water animation 
- Press [e] to complete water animation 
- Press [r] to reset water animation 
- Press [s] to save the current image 
- Press [h] for a high-res output suitable for printing 
- Press [b] to toggle the border

metadata.name: Device 1 #106
metadata.description:
No text to be read, only characters.

Keys

t : TXT snapshot
p : PNG snapshot 2×
P : PNG snapshot 4×
f : Toggle framerate
↑ : Increase scale
↓ : Decrease scale
0 : Auto scale
i : Toggle info
